channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: A Fly-tip Large - More than 10 black bags problem has been found, 20th July
item.description:
A Fly-tip Large - More than 10 black bags problem has been found by Brent Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cambridge Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Alleyway 62, 20th July
item.description:
Bags of waste dumped on verge along with some pots of paint. One section of the pathway in this alleyway has rubbish scattered, and with the amount of waste, this would be difficult for a buggy and/or person in a wheelchair to pass. This alleyway seems to be a constant problem with illegal dumping, especially of household waste.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Unit 1, Leigh Place, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped in front of my property, 20th July
item.description:
Dumped rubbish.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Reedham Street, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins overflowing and flytipping 56 Crampton St, 20th July
item.description:
Bins overflowing and Veolia not emptied all bins. Litter and waste everywhere.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 85, Crampton Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned damaged vehicle, 20th July
item.description:
There is a severely damaged vehicle abandoned outside 174 Tachbrook Street for over five months. It is not just a parking issue; it is an abandoned vehicle hazard. It shows key indicators of abandonment: - Significant physical damage and structural unworthiness. - Stationary for an extended period (5+ months clearly qualifies). - Flat tyres I have twice reported it on the WCC portal and three times raised it with a Cllr who replied that it would be dealt with. My local traffic warden says it has a valid disabled permit so there is nothing he can do, even though at one point the bay was suspended. What will it take for WCC to deal with this?

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter pick needed, 20th July
item.description:
Lawn area at the front of the building in our estate needs litter removal Please ask our cleaner to attend SE15 6RU Thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glengall Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Expired scaffolding permit, 20th July
item.description:
This scaffolding has been blocking the pavement for years at: 25-29 Shore Road The permits keep expiring.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Well Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish on floor, 20th July
item.description:
A group of individuals use this spot for daily hang outs. Starts from 6pm till 12/1am in the night. They convene drinking alcohol, spitting out sunflower seeds on the floor, and leaving rubbish like shown in the pictures daily. They have been asked if they can live but they refuse

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rippolson Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of trash and litter all around estate, 20th July
item.description:
Bins were overflowing and now there’s trash and litter everywhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Valette Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Green LBB bag needs removing in Sussex Road, 20th July

item.title: Abandoned bikes, 20th July
item.description:
Parked in car park, one for three days. Another parked on pavement beside. There is a docking bay in Champion Hill a little way along the road but the riders can't be bothered to dock them. A pain. Lazy. Irresponsible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Champion Hill, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
